threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: Talking about the Tube like it’s always on time gives you away instantly; even locals know it’s a waiting game, not a schedule.
Last night on the 35, a guy was trying to convince everyone that his pet ferret was a therapy animal. Got off at Stockwell, still dubious.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I found a random pop-up bar in an abandoned warehouse, where someone was painting murals and the music was so loud it shook the walls.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg will you find a spontaneous techno party in an abandoned building, complete with art installations and a pasta bar. It's wild.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell who’s local when they flinch at the BART delays but still trust it over an Uber surge. It’s like a badge of honor.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a full tuxedo on the 38 Geary, sipping a coffee like it was a Paris café. Just another Tuesday in the city.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about the parks and plazas, but it’s the little corner bakeries that keep us going. Fresh pa amb tomàquet in the morning is our real secret.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot a recent arrival by their struggle with the metro ticket machines—still fumbling with cash instead of the handy T-Casual.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look confused at the bus schedules. Locals know it never arrives on time, but we can tell you memorized the times anyway.
Caught a late night 196 bus, and a guy was singing opera to the driver. Not even a hint of embarrassment, just pure Brixton charm.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I once saw a kid in a pink bicycle helmet riding a goat down Karl-Marx-Straße. That's when I knew I was really part of this wild neighborhood.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you see a guy selling homemade sauerkraut from a bike trailer while a flock of pigeons dances to techno beats.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a true local by how they know to avoid the BART delays and just roll with a bike or scooter when the Civic Center station closes unexpectedly.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy dressed as a pirate trying to board a crowded bus. He didn’t get on but made the entire line laugh. Only in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists always assume Gràcia is just about the Sagrada Família; they miss the hidden gardens behind the local schools where families gather.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by how they obsessively take photos of every building, especially Gaudí's, while locals just speedwalk past them.
